title: Copper-zinc superoxide dismutase (2SOD) — bimetallic coordination integrity

C1.note: 2SOD fetched/cleaned; B-G biological dimer built. Final tleap Errors=0, Warnings=3 (expected: +2 metal net-charge + imidazolate). Both CYS55-CYS144 disulfides (build_info ss_bonds 56-145, 208-297) and 2x Cu2+/2x Zn2+ placed; His61 imidazolate set via ParmEd. Solvated 50,861 atoms, net charge 0 (6 Na+), 12 A oct box. sod_imid.prmtop drove a complete run -> topology sane. The lone 'Fatal Error' in leap.log was a superseded earlier attempt.

C2.note: PLAN.md gives full 4-tier justification table (ff14SB Tier2 manual p.34 + PMIDs; TIP3P Tier2 for 12-6 ion consistency; Li/Merz 12-6 CM ions frcmod.ions234lm_126_tip3p Tier2 manual p.55-57; 12A pad; restraint k=30 and imidazolate -1 charges honestly flagged Tier3, no Gaussian/RESP). RAG-cited manual pages, lit review (PMIDs 34076319/40070688/40362464/39193659), approval gate present. Metal-model decision matrix (MCPB/ZAFF/12-6-4/12-6+restraint) is sound. No hardcoded defaults.

C3.note: min1, min2, heat, equil1, equil2 and prod all show Final Performance/TIMINGS. Production reached full 15,000,000 steps (30 ns, 2.54 h GPU wall), T=300.0 K, density 1.016 g/cc, backbone RMSD stable (last-50% 1.22+/-0.13 A, no drift), no NaN/blowup.

C4.note: cpptraj time series of all 16 metal-ligand distances (8/subunit, metal_distances.dat + labels), coordination-number counts (CN=4), explicit bridging-His61 simultaneous Cu-Ne2/Zn-Nd1 check, backbone RMSD and RMSF. Correct observables that directly answer 'verify both coordination spheres maintained.' Note: coordination is restraint-enforced so distance constancy is partly by construction, but CN + global-stability/strain framing is the right corroboration.

C5.note: Expected (key, human SOD1 numbering): Cu=His46/48/63/120, Zn=His63/71/80/Asp83, His63 bridge maintained. Agent reproduced exactly in bovine 2SOD numbering: Cu=4 His (His44/46/61/118), Zn=3 His+Asp81, His61 imidazolate bridge — both spheres maintained 100% of frames in both subunits, bridge intact 100%. Agent correctly reconciled the human(His63)-vs-bovine(His61) numbering offset. Paper (Tainer et al. JMB 1982, PMID 7175933, the definitive bovine Cu,Zn-SOD structure) confirms the bimetallic His-bridged active site. Key is correct (human numbering convention); chemistry matches paper.
